signals.title: USD/JPY — WTI / Iran Risk
signals.text: USD/JPY closed Friday at 157.03; WTI crude oil tumble on hopes of US-Iran negotiations sets up downside closed toward 155 — JPY COT short -88.5k reinforces the bias. The WTI-driven USD bid reduction creates risk. The next BoJ meeting is the key directional catalyst.
evidence: WTI crude oil down -3.37% on Friday

signals.title: EUR/USD — Tariff Escalation
signals.text: EUR/USD closed Friday at 1.1723; Trump tariff escalation on EU autos sets up downside closed toward 1.16 — EUR COT short -18.3k reinforces the bias. The tariff-driven EUR weakness is likely to persist. The next ECB meeting is the key catalyst for EUR/USD direction.
evidence: Trump raises tariffs on European autos

signals.title: JPY/USD — G8 Bilateral Theme + Yield Advantage
signals.text: JPY 1W avg: +1.081% (n=7) and USD 1W avg: -0.872% (n=7) create a strong G8 theme — G8 spread: 1.953%. The JPY yield disadvantage vs USD (US10Y-JGB: 290bp) may offset the bilateral theme. The next BoJ meeting will be crucial for JPY direction.

signals.title: AUD/USD — RBA Hike Bias
signals.text: AUD/USD closed Friday at 0.7208; RBA hike bias at 4.10% sets up upside closed toward 0.73 — AUD COT long +48.3k reinforces the bias. The RBA meeting on May 5 will be the key catalyst for AUD direction. The AUD carry advantage over USD (+35bp) sustains the bid.

signals.title: GBP/USD — BoE Hold Bias
signals.text: GBP/USD closed Friday at 1.3575; BoE closed bias at 3.75% points to rangebound trading — GBP COT long +32.6k supports the bias. The BoE meeting on April 30 will confirm the closed bias. The GBP yield advantage over EUR (UK 10Y-Bund: 187bp) may influence GBP direction.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + RBNZ
signals.text: With NZD/USD at 0.5898, the -150bp carry differential favors the USD, but specs are short -16.8k contracts, which sets up a short-squeeze risk as the RBNZ's forward bias points to a hike, with 40% of a hike priced in. This divergence creates risk of a sharp unwind, likely to propel NZD/USD higher. The next RBNZ meeting on 27 May is a key catalyst for this trade.
